official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, and decode the chunk_batch_finished packet body
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound chunk_batch_finished frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns chunk_batch_finished immediately after change_difficulty.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the chunk_batch_finished id to ClientboundChunkBatchFinishedPacket.
The official chunk_batch_finished body is batchSize encoded as a FriendlyByteBuf VarInt.
The fixture uses official ClientboundChunkBatchFinishedPacket(int), so it does not require an initialized Level, chunk storage, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded batchSize, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove chunk batch pacing behavior, chunk-load completion, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer chunk batch pacing behavior, chunk-load completion, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
